description.value:
A slender silver circlet, unadorned. The kind noblemen wore in an older age, before the styles changed and such things fell out of fashion. It was found in an estate sale, the previous owner having died without heirs.

Curse. The wearer cannot be crowned, titled, or formally recognised as a leader while wearing it. Any ceremony attempting to confer rank, command, or authority on the wearer fails - the words won't come out right, papers are unsigned, witnesses forget. The wearer also has disadvantage on Intimidation checks made against those of higher social standing.

unidentified.description: A slender silver circlet. Plain. Elegant in a quiet way. Worth something to the right collector.

cursedMeta.decoyAppearance: A slender silver circlet. Tasteful. Valuable to collectors.
cursedMeta.trueNature: Cannot receive formal rank or authority while worn. Disadvantage on Intimidation vs higher social rank.

curse.name: The Hollow Crown
curse.description: While worn the bearer cannot be crowned, titled, or formally recognised as a leader; ceremonies that confer authority fail. They also have disadvantage on Intimidation checks against those of higher social standing.

hints: Official seals smudge only for this bearer; heralds stumble over their titles.

escalation.effect: Courtesy titles feel awkward on the tongue; small snubs from staff.
